core.label: Lua Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, tables, and basic program structure.

topics.label: Installation & REPL
topics.description: Install Lua and use the interactive interpreter. Lua is tiny (~300KB) and embeds easily.

topics.label: Variables & Types
topics.description: nil, boolean, number, string, function, table, userdata, thread. Lua has 8 types and dynamic typing.

topics.label: Strings
topics.description: String concatenation (..), length (#), and string library functions. Strings are immutable.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/elseif/else/end, while, repeat/until, for (numeric and generic), break. No switch/case.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: First-class functions, multiple returns, variadic (...), and closures. Functions are values in Lua.

topics.label: Tables
topics.description: The only data structure — arrays, dictionaries, objects, and modules are all tables.

topics.label: Tables as Arrays
topics.description: 1-based indexed tables with #, table.insert, table.remove, table.sort.

topics.label: Iterators (pairs, ipairs)
topics.description: Iterate tables with pairs (all keys) and ipairs (sequential integer keys).

topics.label: Local vs Global
topics.description: Variables are global by default — use local for scoping. Always use local.

topics.label: Error Handling
topics.description: pcall, xpcall, error, and assert. Lua uses protected calls instead of try/catch.

topics.label: Metatables & Metamethods
topics.description: Customize table behavior with __index, __newindex, __add, __tostring, etc. Metatables power Lua's OOP.

topics.label: OOP with Metatables
topics.description: Implement classes and inheritance using tables and __index chains. Lua has no built-in class system.

topics.label: Modules & require
topics.description: Create and load modules with require. Modules are tables returned from Lua files.

topics.label: Closures & Upvalues
topics.description: Functions capture local variables from enclosing scopes. Closures are Lua's primary encapsulation tool.

topics.label: Coroutines
topics.description: Cooperative multitasking with coroutine.create, resume, yield. Lua coroutines are stackful.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: Lua patterns (simpler than regex) with string.find, string.match, string.gmatch, string.gsub.

topics.label: File I/O
topics.description: Read and write files with io.open, io.read, io.write. Simple file handle API.

topics.label: C API Basics
topics.description: Embed Lua in C/C++ applications and call C from Lua. The C API is Lua's primary integration point.
